tasks.subject: [site-feature-catalog] [P1] Create features data model and initial catalog
tasks.description:
Create the features data model file and populate it with the initial catalog of 13 features across all 5 categories.

**File to create:** `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/features.ts`

**Implementation:**

1. Define the `FeatureStatus` type:
```typescript
export type FeatureStatus = 'ga' | 'beta' | 'coming-soon';
```

2. Define the `FeatureCategory` type:
```typescript
export type FeatureCategory = 'console' | 'pulse' | 'relay' | 'mesh' | 'core';
```

3. Define the `CATEGORY_LABELS` constant:
```typescript
export const CATEGORY_LABELS: Record<FeatureCategory, string> = {
  console: 'Console',
  pulse: 'Pulse',
  relay: 'Relay',
  mesh: 'Mesh',
  core: 'Core',
};
```

4. Define the `Feature` interface with all fields:
   - `slug: string` — URL key, immutable, lowercase-kebab
   - `name: string` — Display name
   - `category: FeatureCategory` — Subsystem grouping
   - `tagline: string` — Benefit one-liner ≤80 chars
   - `description: string` — Meta-description ready, 120-160 chars, problem-first
   - `status: FeatureStatus` — Lifecycle stage
   - `featured?: boolean` — If true, appears on homepage (max 6)
   - `benefits: string[]` — 3-5 concrete capability statements, ≤12 words each
   - `media?: { screenshot?: string; demoUrl?: string; alt?: string }` — Optional media
   - `mdxSlug?: string` — Deferred MDX integration placeholder
   - `docsUrl?: string` — Explicit docs link
   - `relatedFeatures?: string[]` — Cross-link slugs
   - `sortOrder?: number` — Display order within category

   Every exported function/type must have TSDoc per the documentation rules.

5. Define the `features` const array with all 13 features from the spec, organized by category:
   - **Console (4):** chat-interface, tool-approval, question-prompts, file-uploads
   - **Pulse (1):** pulse-scheduler
   - **Relay (3):** relay-message-bus, slack-adapter, telegram-adapter
   - **Mesh (2):** mesh-agent-discovery, mesh-topology
   - **Core (3):** mcp-server, cli, tunnel

   The exact feature data is specified in the spec Section 1. Copy all slug, name, category, tagline, description, status, featured, benefits, docsUrl, relatedFeatures, and sortOrder values verbatim.

   Key data constraints:
   - Exactly 6 features have `featured: true`: chat-interface, pulse-scheduler, relay-message-bus, mesh-agent-discovery, mesh-topology, mcp-server
   - All taglines ≤80 chars and benefit-led
   - All descriptions 120-160 chars and problem-first
   - Benefits arrays have 3-5 items, each ≤12 words
   - Array sorted by category then sortOrder

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] File compiles with zero TypeScript errors
- [ ] All 13 features present with correct data
- [ ] All 5 categories represented
- [ ] Exactly 6 features marked `featured: true`
- [ ] All slugs are unique
- [ ] All `relatedFeatures` references resolve to valid slugs in the array
- [ ] TSDoc on all exported types and constants
tasks.activeForm: Creating features data model and populating initial catalog

tasks.description:
Update the marketing feature barrel `index.ts` to export the new features data, types, and components.

**File to modify:** `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/index.ts`

Add the following exports after the existing `// Data` section:

```typescript
// Feature catalog
export { features, CATEGORY_LABELS } from './lib/features';
export type { Feature, FeatureStatus, FeatureCategory } from './lib/features';

// Feature catalog components
export { FeatureCard } from './ui/FeatureCard';
```

Note: `FeatureCatalogSection` will be added to the barrel in a later task (2.3) when it is created.

The barrel file follows the existing pattern: data exports grouped with data, component exports grouped with UI components, type exports grouped with types. Place the feature catalog data exports after the existing `// Data` group and the component export after the existing `// UI components — homepage` group.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `features`, `CATEGORY_LABELS` are importable from `@/layers/features/marketing`
- [ ] `Feature`, `FeatureStatus`, `FeatureCategory` types are importable
- [ ] `FeatureCard` is importable
- [ ] No circular dependency issues
- [ ] Barrel file compiles cleanly

tasks.subject: [site-feature-catalog] [P2] Add feature pages to sitemap.ts
tasks.description:
Update the sitemap generator to include the `/features` catalog index and all individual `/features/[slug]` pages.

**File to modify:** `apps/site/src/app/sitemap.ts`

**Changes:**

1. Add import at the top of the file:
```typescript
import { features } from '@/layers/features/marketing';
```

2. Add two new sitemap entry groups in the function body, before the return statement:
```typescript
const featureCatalogPage: MetadataRoute.Sitemap = [
  {
    url: `${BASE_URL}/features`,
    lastModified: new Date(),
    changeFrequency: 'monthly',
    priority: 0.7,
  },
];

const featurePages: MetadataRoute.Sitemap = features.map((feature) => ({
  url: `${BASE_URL}/features/${feature.slug}`,
  lastModified: new Date(),
  changeFrequency: 'monthly' as const,
  priority: 0.8,
}));
```

3. Update the return statement to include the new entries:
```typescript
return [...staticPages, ...featureCatalogPage, ...featurePages, ...docPages, ...blogPages];
```

**Key details:**
- Individual feature pages get priority 0.8 (higher than the catalog index at 0.7, same as docs)
- Catalog index gets priority 0.7 (same as doc section pages)
- `changeFrequency: 'monthly'` — feature pages change infrequently
- The `as const` on monthly in the map callback is needed for TypeScript to narrow the literal type

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` includes `/features` entry with priority 0.7
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` includes all 13 `/features/[slug]` entries with priority 0.8
- [ ] Existing sitemap entries (static, docs, blog) are unaffected
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly

tasks.subject: [site-feature-catalog] [P2] Add features section to llms.txt route
tasks.description:
Update the `llms.txt` route to include a `## Features` section listing all features for AI crawlers.

**File to modify:** `apps/site/src/app/llms.txt/route.ts`

**Changes:**

1. Add import at the top of the file (after existing imports):
```typescript
import { features } from '@/layers/features/marketing/lib/features';
```

2. Add a new section builder function (after the existing `buildCapabilitiesSection`):
```typescript
function buildFeaturesSection(): string {
  return features
    .map((f) => `- **${f.name}** (${f.category}): ${f.tagline}`)
    .join('\n');
}
```

3. Update the GET() template string to add the `## Features` section between `## Core Capabilities` and `## Documentation`:

The template should become:
```
## Core Capabilities

${buildCapabilitiesSection()}

## Features

${buildFeaturesSection()}

## Documentation
...
```

**Key details:**
- Each feature entry format: `- **Feature Name** (category): tagline`
- Section placed after Core Capabilities (high-level subsystems) and before Documentation (reference links)
- Uses the `features` import from the lib path (not the barrel) to match the existing `subsystems` import pattern in the file

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `/llms.txt` contains a `## Features` section
- [ ] All 13 features are listed with name, category, and tagline
- [ ] Section appears between Core Capabilities and Documentation
- [ ] Existing sections (Core Capabilities, Documentation, Blog, Links) unchanged
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly
tasks.activeForm: Adding features section to llms.txt route

tasks.subject: [site-feature-catalog] [P2] Create FeatureCatalogSection homepage teaser and integrate into homepage
tasks.description:
Create the `FeatureCatalogSection` component for the homepage teaser and integrate it into the marketing homepage and navigation. **File to create:** `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/ui/FeatureCatalogSection.tsx` ```typescript import Link from 'next/link'; import { ArrowRight } from 'lucide-react'; import { features } from '../lib/features'; import { FeatureCard } from './FeatureCard'; /** * Homepage teaser section — shows featured features in a grid with * a link to the full /features catalog. */ export function FeatureCatalogSection() { const featuredFeatures = features.filter((f) => f.featured); return (

Built for how you actually work

Every subsystem designed to get out of the way.

All features
{featuredFeatures.map((feature) => ( ))}
View all features
); } ``` **Key design details:** - Filters to only `featured: true` features (exactly 6) - Desktop: "All features" link visible in header row, hidden on mobile - Mobile: "View all features" link visible below grid, hidden on desktop - Uses the same `FeatureCard` component as the catalog page - Section heading: "Built for how you actually work" (benefit-led, brand voice) - Subheading: "Every subsystem designed to get out of the way." **File to modify:** `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/index.ts` Add export for the new component: ```typescript export { FeatureCatalogSection } from './ui/FeatureCatalogSection'; ``` **File to modify:** `apps/site/src/app/(marketing)/page.tsx` 1. Add `FeatureCatalogSection` to the import from `@/layers/features/marketing`: ```typescript import { // ... existing imports ... SubsystemsSection, FeatureCatalogSection, HonestySection, // ... } from '@/layers/features/marketing'; ``` 2. Insert `` in the JSX between `` and ``: ```tsx ``` 3. Add 'features' as the first nav link: ```typescript const navLinks = [ { label: 'features', href: '/features' }, { label: 'about', href: '#about' }, { label: 'blog', href: '/blog' }, { label: 'docs', href: '/docs' }, ]; ``` **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Homepage shows `FeatureCatalogSection` with exactly 6 featured features - [ ] Section appears between SubsystemsSection and HonestySection - [ ] "All features" link visible on desktop, hidden on mobile - [ ] "View all features" link visible on mobile, hidden on desktop - [ ] Feature cards link to individual feature pages - [ ] "features" appears as first nav link in MarketingNav - [ ] `FeatureCatalogSection` is exported from the marketing barrel - [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly across all modified files

[site-feature-catalog] [P3] Verify full TypeScript compilation and build
tasks.description:
Run TypeScript type-checking and the full site build to verify all new files compile cleanly and pages generate correctly.

**Steps:**

1. Run TypeScript type-checking across the monorepo:
```bash
pnpm typecheck
```

2. Run the site build to verify static generation:
```bash
dotenv -- turbo build --filter=@dorkos/site
```

3. Verify that `generateStaticParams` generates all 13 feature slugs by checking the build output for `/features/[slug]` entries.

4. Run ESLint to verify no linting violations:
```bash
pnpm lint --filter=@dorkos/site
```

5. Run all tests to verify nothing is broken:
```bash
pnpm vitest run --filter=@dorkos/site
```

**What to check in build output:**
- `/features` page generates as a server-rendered page
- All 13 `/features/[slug]` pages generate as static pages
- All 13 `/features/[slug]/opengraph-image` routes are registered
- No TypeScript errors in any new or modified files
- No ESLint errors (TSDoc, import restrictions, etc.)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- [ ] `turbo build --filter=@dorkos/site` succeeds
- [ ] All 13 feature pages are statically generated
- [ ] ESLint passes with no errors
- [ ] All feature-related tests pass
- [ ] No regressions in existing tests
tasks.activeForm: Verifying full TypeScript compilation and build
